headline: Google won’t just admit it’s feeding YouTube creators to its music AI
summary: Independent musicians are suing Google for allegedly using their YouTube uploads. They claim their songs trained Google's Lyria music AI without consent. This lawsuit highlights growing concerns over AI training data ethics.

headline: You can just tell the Instagram algorithm what you want now
summary: Instagram is launching a 'Your Algorithm' feature for enhanced user control. Users can now view and modify topics of interest for their main feed. This aims to personalize content recommendations and improve user experience.

headline: Amazon’s Echo speakers can now help kids wind down and fall asleep
summary: Amazon introduced 'Sleep Studio' for Echo and Echo Kids smart speakers. This feature offers bedtime stories, relaxing sounds, and guided meditations. It aims to make bedtime easier for children and parents.

headline: Microsoft restricts Claude Fable for employees over data retention concerns
summary: Microsoft is limiting internal use of Anthropic's new Claude Fable 5 AI model. The restriction stems from concerns over Anthropic's data retention requirements. This contrasts with its quick rollout to GitHub Copilot customers.

headline: Ubisoft Closing 2 More Studios Amid Further Cuts and Job Losses
summary: Ubisoft will shut down two additional development studios. These closures are part of ongoing cost-cutting measures by the company. The move signals further job losses within the gaming giant.

headline: The Apple AirPods Pro 3 Earbuds Drops to the Lowest Price Ever Ahead of Father's Day
summary: Apple AirPods Pro 3 earbuds are now at their lowest price ever on Amazon. Shoppers can save $70 compared to buying directly from Apple. This significant discount arrives just in time for Father's Day.

headline: Obsession Breakout Star Inde Navarrette Goes Viral for Old Streams of the Last of Us, PlayStation Responds
summary: Inde Navarrette, star of 'Obsession', has gone viral for old gaming livestreams. Her streams include playing Sony's hit title, 'The Last of Us'. PlayStation has reportedly acknowledged her newfound viral popularity.

headline: PlayStation Plus Game Catalog for June 2026 Headlined by Final Fantasy 16 and Kingdom Come: Deliverance
summary: PlayStation Plus subscribers can expect a strong lineup for June 2026. The Game Catalog additions are headlined by 'Final Fantasy 16'. 'Kingdom Come: Deliverance' is also a major inclusion for the month.

headline: 'I'll never get over watching my home burn': Belfast residents reel after night of unrest
summary: Belfast residents are reeling after a night of severe unrest saw homes burned. Protests erupted following a knife attack in the city's north. Many were forced to flee their residences amid the chaos.

headline: Three arrested over Glasgow unrest and racist assaults after Belfast knife attack
summary: Three individuals have been arrested in Glasgow following unrest and alleged racist assaults. Police Scotland reported victims were attacked 'because of the colour of their skin'. The incidents occurred after a knife attack in Belfast.

headline: Five jailed for violence at Henry Nowak police protest
summary: Five individuals have been imprisoned for violence during a police protest. The disorder took place in Southampton, near where 18-year-old Henry Nowak was murdered. This follows significant unrest related to the case.

headline: Trump says US will hit Iran 'hard' again on Wednesday
summary: Former US President Trump warned the US would strike Iran 'hard' again. He stated Iran must 'pay the price' for delaying a deal. Tehran had previously vowed retaliation against any attacks.
